input.background: A survey was conducted to test the hypothesis that men with deep singing voices are likely to have higher levels of testosterone than men with higher voices. Professional and student male singers were asked about the number of brothers and sisters they had. The results for 195 singers with siblings are presented in a table. The author of the report performed a Chi-squared test and drew conclusions about the relationship between voice groups and sibling sex ratios.
input.question: What feature of the data breaks a fundamental assumption of all Chi-squared tests?

output.answer: The observations are not independent, as the 422 siblings related to only 195 singers. Large families will carry more influence than small ones.
